task_completion.description: The agent opens the broken mission file to begin repairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show QGroundControl (or a text editor) open with the file 'incoming_mission.plan' loaded, displaying the mission waypoints.

task_completion.description: The agent inspects the mission items to find the waypoints with incorrect altitudes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks through or scrolls to mission items (specifically around indices 1, 3, and 4) that have altitudes significantly different from 50m.

task_completion.description: The agent updates the waypoints so they all use the standard survey altitude of 50m.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The altitude input fields for the modified waypoints in the mission list are changed to '50' (or a value between 40 and 60).

task_completion.description: The agent adds a Return to Launch (RTL) command at the end of the mission sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Return to Launch' (RTL) or 'Return' mission item is visible at the very bottom of the mission item list.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the corrected mission file with the required new filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog, file manager, or application menu shows the file being saved as 'fixed_mission.plan' in the /home/ga/Documents/QGC/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical interface (like QGroundControl or a GUI text editor) to modify the mission plan, rather than bypassing interaction with terminal commands like 'sed' or 'jq'.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the QGroundControl interface or a text editor window open with the mission file, with cursor/mouse interactions visible.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the altitude values and added the RTL command manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate states where the altitude is being edited in the UI (e.g., typing '50' into the altitude field) and the RTL command being selected from a dropdown or menu.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely saved the new file rather than just renaming a pre-existing file in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file save dialog or application save interface shows the user typing or confirming the name 'fixed_mission.plan'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The broken mission file is located at /home/ga/Documents/QGC/incoming_mission.plan. The waypoints with incorrect altitudes are at indices 1, 3, and 4. The target altitude for all waypoints is 50m (acceptable tolerance is 40m-60m). The mission must end with a Return to Launch (RTL) command (MAV_CMD=20). The final repaired file must be saved exactly as /home/ga/Documents/QGC/fixed_mission.plan.
